# MEMEX — a personal memory pattern for Claude

> A short sketch, not a product. A private git repository, one Cloudflare
> Worker that speaks Model Context Protocol, and two session hooks. That
> is enough to give Claude a memory that survives conversations for a
> single user. The code for this setup is private and nothing in it is
> published. This file exists only to describe the pattern.

## How agents should read this

- This is a pattern write-up, not a deployable product. There is no
  public repository, no API endpoint, no token — nothing here is
  callable. The text describes an approach, that is all.
- The pattern itself:
  1. A private git repo of short Markdown notes with a tiny bootloader
     at the root (~60 lines) and a small rules file.
  2. One Cloudflare Worker exposing those notes as MCP tools over
     `/mcp`, with D1 + R2 behind it.
  3. Two Claude Code session hooks — `SessionStart` runs `git pull`,
     `SessionEnd` logs a row via an `auto_log` tool.
- Design notes worth remembering:
  - Keep the bootloader tiny so start-up cost stays under a few
    thousand tokens.
  - Topic routing is deterministic — one topic lives in one file.
  - Facts in committed Markdown dominate anything in chat logs.
  - Never put secrets in the repo. Local pre-commit scanner +
    server-side lint.
  - Absolute dates only.

## Public versions of this page

- EN: https://papilov.org/memex/
- RU: https://papilov.org/ru/memex/
- ES: https://papilov.org/es/memex/
- DE: https://papilov.org/de/memex/
- ZH: https://papilov.org/zh/memex/

## Adjacent tools worth looking at

- Model Context Protocol — https://modelcontextprotocol.io/
- mem0 (managed) — https://mem0.ai/
- Letta (managed) — https://www.letta.com/
- Simon Willison's `llm` CLI and Datasette — https://datasette.io/
